transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yeah.
What is your main medical concern today?
I've been feeling rather depressed.
When did your feelings of depression start?
I don't know. Like, 3 weeks ago, I guess. I don't know. Maybe 3 months ago. So
Can you describe any other symptoms you have been experiencing, such as changes in sleep, appetite, energy, or thoughts?
I have lost my appetite. My sleep is horrible.
Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself or ending your life?
And I'm just down all the time.
Have you had any thoughts about harming yourself or ending your life?
No.
Have you noticed any changes in your concentration, interest in activities, or energy levels?
Yeah. Everything is bad. I'm not interested in anything that I like to do, and my energy is in the dump.
Have you experienced any recent major life changes, stressors, or losses that might be contributing to how you feel?
I don't know. I started a new job, but it's really nothing dramatically different than the old job. I just missed some of my coworkers.
Have you ever been diagnosed with depression or received treatment for it before?
No.
Have you noticed any physical symptoms like weight changes, pain, or changes in bowel habits?
No. Not really.
Any history of anxiety, bipolar disorder, or other mental health conditions in yourself or your family?
Oh, my family has a lot of mental health issues, anxiety, depression,
Have you noticed any changes in your mood throughout the day, such as feeling worse in the morning or evening?
I feel bad. Morning evening, night, noon, whatever. All the time.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
No.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Male.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you’ve been feeling down for a few weeks with low mood, low energy, poor sleep, and loss of interest in things you usually enjoy. Based on what you told me, this may be a form of depression that could benefit from medication treatment. A licensed physician will review this note and, if appropriate, may prescribe medication like sertraline or bupropion. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t do an exam or tests, so if your symptoms get worse, especially if you start having thoughts of harming yourself, please seek urgent in-person care right away. Otherwise, if you need an in-person exam or testing, you can go to urgent care or see your primary care clinician. A doctor will also want to check for other possible causes with some lab tests. Take care, and remember help is available.
